steps.title: Factor the expression
steps.content: Inside the parentheses, $x^2-100y^2$ is a difference of squares: $(x-10y)(x+10y)$. Therefore:$$14(x^2-100y^2)=14(x-10y)(x+10y)$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The factored form is $14(x-10y)(x+10y)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
